case_number: Civil Court having jurisdiction and not

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. C.P. No.D-1420 of 2020 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on MA-7202/2020 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For orders on MA-7203/2020. 4. For hearing of main case. 16.12.2020 Mrs. Razia Ali Zaman, advocate for petitioner. = 1. Urgency granted. 2. Deferred. 3. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 4. It is contended by learned counsel for the petitioner that the petitioner has been denied right of inheritance in property left by his father, his application pending with the Revenue officials for cancellation of existing entries in Revenue record and declaring him to be legal heir of his father Allah Warayo is pending decision before the Revenue authorities since long and they may be directed to dispose of the same in accordance with law. The direction sought for by the petitioner could hardly be issued by this Court in exercise of its constitutional jurisdiction simply for the reason that the cancellation of the existing entries in the Revenue record and declaring the petitioner to be one of legal heir need probe by recording evidence and such exercise could only be undertaken by Civil Court having jurisdiction and not by Revenue authorities. Consequently, the instant petition being misconceived is dismissed in limine together with the listed applications with an advise to the petitioner to approach the Civil Court having jurisdiction for the purpose. J U D G E J U D G E Ahmed/Pa,
